rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JASON HEYMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 4, 2018, Line 2, RESPONDENT TPG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, L.P.'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. TPG's motion to vacate the voluntary dismissal without prejudice entered on March 8, 2018 is GRANTED. This case concerned the propriety of the subpoena and when the court denied the motion to compel, the case was effectively over. At that point, a judgment of dismissal with prejudice in favor of TPG was inevitable and Heyman's right to file a voluntary dismissal was cut off because the "trial" already commenced within the meaning of CCP 581. (Law Offices of Andre L. Ellis v. Yang (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 869, 877.) TPG shall prepare, file, and serve a judgment of dismissal with prejudice in its favor against petitioners forthwith. TPG's request for fess under CCP 1987.2 is DENIED. First, TPG sought expenses when opposing the discovery motion and the court issued an order that did not award fees. TPG is effectively bringing an improper motion for reconsideration that does not comply with CCP 1008. Second, the court finds that Heyman acted with substantial justification and therefore, an award of fees is not warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for TPG is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JASON HEYMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Motion To Strike And Tax Tpg Capital Management L.P.S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 4, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONERS JASON HEYMAN, MARTIN LESAK, DOMINIC NUCIFORO'S Petitioners Motion To Strike And Tax Tpg Capital Management L.P.S. Heyman's motion to tax is GRANTED IN PART. The court strikes $945.16 in travel expenses. "The only travel expenses authorized by section 1033.5 are those to attend depositions. (§ 1033.5, subd. (a)(3).)" ( Ladas v. CSAA (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 775.) TPG's distinction between local and non-local travel expenses does not change this rule. The court denies the motion as to the e-filing fees, which TPG substantiates in its opposition. The court denies Heyman's motion to tax the entire costs memo because it was served by e-mail. Heyman received the memo and was able to attack it. In addition, the court plans to vacate Heyman's dismissal without prejudice and enter a new judgment for dismissal with prejudice. TPG would be able to file and mail-serve a new costs memorandum after entry of the new dismissal. "The law neither does nor requires idle acts" and the court addresses the merits of the claimed costs. (CC 3532.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Heyman is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RAUL VASQUEZ VS. FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 4, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication. Defendants Farmers Insurance Group, Truck Insurance Exchange, and Fire Insurance Exchange's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. The court grants summary adjudication on the cause of action for breach of contract based on Truck's commercial insurance policy. Vasquez does not dispute that the policy included a cooperation provision. (Defendants' UMF 4; Parkhurst Dec., Ex. A, sec. I.E.3.a.) The undisputed evidence shows that on 2/7/13, defendants requested additional documents. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. D.) Vasquez failed to respond to this letter and violated the cooperation provision as a matter of law. The requested information was material and defendants were prejudiced. "Where the insurer has reason to suspect arson, it is relevant and material to inquire into the financial condition of the insured¿"(Abdelhamid v. Fire Ins. Exch. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 990, 1001.) The letter sought recent tax statements and Vasquez failed to provide them, which hampered the investigation. Vasquez cites his declaration to argue that he provided all documents that Francis orally requested. Defendants correctly note that Vasquez's declaration is inadmissible because it does not comply with CCP 2015.5 and the court sustains defendants' objection to that declaration. In addition, Vasquez's claim on the commercial policy is excluded under the dishonesty exclusion. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. A sec. I.B.1.f.) Vasquez was running an illegal auto body shop. The court grants summary adjudication on the cause of action for breach of contract based on the Fire Insurance Exchange's homeowner's policy. The policy provides that Vasquez has one-year to file a claim. (Sanck Dec., Ex. A, Condition 11.) Vasquez does not dispute this fact. (Defendants' UMF 14.) The undisputed evidence demonstrates that Vasquez first made a claim on 11/18/15. (Sanck Dec., Ex. E.) Therefore, the claim is untimely. Vasquez argues that defendants are estopped from raising the time bar issue because a letter dated 2/11/13 refers to the statute of limitations expiring on 11/21/15. (Parkhurst Dec., Ex. E, pg. 3.) That letter related to the commercial policy, not the homeowners policy. Vasquez does not dispute that "Farmers Insurance Group" is not a legal entity. (Defendants' UMF 44.) Therefore, Farmers Insurance Group is not a proper party to this case and summary judgment is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LUZ AL-DIJAILI ET AL VS. SHREE JALARAMBAPA HOTEL, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, May 4, 2018, Line 4, Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Luz Al-Dulii's motion to compel compliance with a third party deposition subpoena for production of business records is denied. There is no showing that the third party was served with the motion to compel and given an opportunity to oppose. This is without prejudice to plaintiff's right to renew the motion once appropriate notice is given. Counsel for the moving party should prepare an order reflecting the court's ruling and present it to the court at the hearing on May 4, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ROMWALD CONNOLLY ET AL VS. MICHAEL D. MOORE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Reasonable Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, May 4, 2018, Line 2, Cross-Defendants Romwald Connolly and Desmond Morgan's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Reasonable Attorneys Fees. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cross-defendants Romwald Connolly and Desmond Morgan's motion to compel production of documents (set one) is granted as follows; 1. Cross-complainant Moores's objections to request for production no. 11 and requests for production nos. 1 -4 dated January 19, 2018 are overruled and cross-complainant is ordered to produce within thirty (30) days from the date of this order all documents responsive to those requests. To the extent that cross-complainant is withholding any documents on the grounds of privilege such documents must be identified with sufficient detail to allow cross-defendants to challenge any claim of privilege. Police reports and related documents, not prepared by or under the directions of counsel are not work product. Additionally, documents in the possession of cross-complainant's counsel, but not personally in the possession of cross-defendant must be produced unless subject to a privilege claim. 2. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $1,000. Is assessed against counter-claimant and his attorneys, payment of which must be made to cross-defendants within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Counsel for cross-defendants should prepare an appropriate order, submit to counsel for cross-complaints for approval as to form and deliver to the court at the hearing on May 4, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)
